title: Publications | Stats NZ
content:
## Value of building work put in place: March 2026 quarter
Value of building work statistics estimate the value and volume of work put in place on construction jobs in New Zealand.

## Livestock slaughtering statistics: April 2026
Livestock slaughtering statistics provide information about kills by region and animal type.

## International trade: March 2026 quarter
International trade statistics provide information on imports and exports of goods and services between New Zealand and our trading partners.

## Building consents issued: April 2026
Building consent statistics are about the number, floor area, and value of new homes, non-residential buildings, and alterations approved for construction.

## Visitor spending in New Zealand boosts services exports
Services exports increased $1.6 billion (17 percent) to $11.5 billion in the March 2026 quarter compared with the March 2025 quarter.

## Multi-unit homes lead rise in home consents in April 2026
There were 39,087 new homes consented in Aotearoa New Zealand in the year ended April 2026, up 16 percent compared with the year ended April 2025.
The population clock estimates New Zealand's population in real time.

## Major upgrade to New Zealand’s largest research database
Future proofing the country’s largest research database will become a focus for Stats NZ as the agency takes further steps to modernise New Zealand’s data system.

## Applying the Stats NZ data updates policy to outputs from the Household Income and Living Survey
Read about how the Stats NZ data updates policy is applied to the Household Income and Living Survey (HILS).

## Comparing direct and indirect seasonal adjustment of gross domestic product (GDP)
Presents a side-by-side comparison of GDP growth rates using direct and indirect seasonal adjustment.

## Applying the Stats NZ data updates policy to outputs from the Household Economic Survey
Looks at how the Stats NZ data updates policy is applied to the Household Economic Survey (HES).

## Monthly consumers price index – updates on progress
Summarises the approach and updates to the development of the monthly consumers price index (CPI).
